channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Heavy Rain Causes Waterlogging in Delhi and LPG Cylinders Swept Away in Maharashtra
item.description: Heavy rainfall continued in Delhi and the National Capital Region (NCR) on Thursday, leading to severe waterlogging and traffic disruptions. The Mehrauli-Badarpur road and Burari area in northern Delh...

item.title: Nigerian Army Soldiers Petition Tinubu Over Delay in Voluntary Discharge
item.description: A group of serving Nigerian Army personnel have petitioned President Bola Tinubu, the National Assembly, and top defense officials over alleged delays in processing voluntary discharge applications. T...

item.title: Bonnie Tyler Passes Away at 75 After Illness, Leaving Unforgettable Legacy
item.description: Bonnie Tyler, the Welsh singer known for her iconic hits like 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' has passed away at 75 after a brief illness. She died on July 8, 2026, in Portugal following emergency surge...

item.title: Susan Collins' Senate Campaign Faces Challenges After Opponent's Exit
item.description: Senator Susan Collins' (R-ME) Senate re-election campaign is in turmoil after Democrat Graham Platner, her primary opponent, abruptly suspended his campaign due to credible allegations of sexual misco...

item.title: Calgary police shooting: Family of woman with mental health struggles speaks out
item.description: The family of Melanie Hambrook, a 53-year-old Edmonton woman fatally shot by Calgary police during a confrontation on Deerfoot Trail in June 2026, has shared their grief and concerns. Adam Vaananen, h...

item.title: News Corp Australia blocks crawler bots with access restrictions
item.description: The article discusses News Corp Australia's measures to manage crawler bot traffic on their websites. Readers or subscribers encountering a block message are advised to check their browser settings, d...

item.title: Patelo and Dee Reappear at Outdoor Lounge After Months of Privacy
item.description: VJ Patelo and his wife Dee have resurfaced after months of keeping a low profile, sparking renewed interest. The couple, who gained fame from their lavish 2025 wedding in Limuru, were spotted at an up...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Visits Quetta for Law and Order Meeting
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif arrived in Quetta for a day-long visit, during which he will chair a high-level meeting to address the law and order situation in Balochistan. The visit, accompanied by k...

item.title: Brits Paying Extra for Overpriced Contracts: Survey Reveals 38% Feel They're Overcharged
item.description: A survey of 2,000 adults reveals 38% of Brits are stuck in contracts they believe overcharge them by £576 annually. Half of respondents noticed rising prices over time, while 26% feel they receive poo...

item.title: Two Separate Shootings Kill Seven, Including Teenagers, in Cape Town
item.description: In Cape Town, two separate shootings occurred on Wednesday night, 8 July 2026, resulting in seven fatalities and multiple injuries. The first incident took place at around 8:10pm in Tafelsig, Mitchell...

item.title: Joe Rogan Criticizes Trump's Iran War Comments
item.description: Celebrity podcaster Joe Rogan expressed intense frustration with President Donald Trump's policies regarding the Iran conflict during an interview with right-wing British politician Rupert Lowe. Rogan...

item.title: Pauline Hanson and Tommy Robinson discuss political strategies in a podcast
item.description: Pauline Hanson, the Australian far-right politician, has recorded a podcast with British far-right activist Tommy Robinson during her visit to the UK for a major conservative political conference. The...

item.title: Successful maritime rescue operation saves 20 Pakistani sailors from sinking cargo vessel
item.description: In a swift and coordinated emergency response, Pakistan Navy collaborated with Pakistan Maritime Security Agency (PMSA) to rescue all 20 Pakistani crew members from a cargo dhow experiencing severe fl...

item.title: Calgary City Addresses Noise Complaints at Stampede Music Festival
item.description: The City of Calgary is actively monitoring noise levels from the annual Stampede music festival following numerous complaints from residents. The issue has sparked concerns about public disturbances, ...

item.title: Novo Nordisk Launches Once-Weekly Insulin Injection Awiqli for Adults with Diabetes in India
item.description: Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk has launched its once-weekly basal insulin injection, Awiqli, in India for the treatment of diabetes in adults. This innovation replaces the need for daily ins...

item.title: Former EALA MP Abubakar Zein Dies in Nairobi After Surgery
item.description: Abubakar Zein, a former member of the East Africa Legislative Assembly (EALA) and key figure in the Wiper party, has passed away in Nairobi after undergoing surgery. He was admitted to Avenue Hospital...

item.title: Right-Wing Mobile Museums Promote Biased History Amid Criticism
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding the 'Freedom Trucks,' a series of mobile museums funded by right-wing groups and aligned with former President Donald Trump's ideological agenda. Thes...

item.title: Huldah Momanyi Launches Kenya-US Student Exchange Program and Environmental Nursery Initiative
item.description: Minnesota State Representative Huldah Momanyi Hitsley unveiled a Kenya-US student exchange program during the launch of Truphena Muthoni’s Hug the Earth Tree Nursery initiative. The nursery, now a sus...

item.title: Ntsiki Mazwai Launches Mayoral Campaign Focused on Service Delivery and Infrastructure
item.description: Media personality and poet Ntsiki Mazwai has officially entered the Johannesburg mayoral race as a candidate for the Land Party, vowing to prioritise service delivery, housing, and infrastructure repa...

item.title: CBN warns against rejecting standard ₦100 note, reiterates legal tender status
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has clarified that the standard ₦100 banknote remains legal tender and warned that rejecting it could lead to enforcement measures. This comes after reports of trader...
